titleStmt.title: SII 25.300: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 300. (A.R. No. 300 of 1908.) TIRUKKAḶITTAṬṬAI, KUMBHAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Vēdapurīśvara temple.
This incomplete inscription may palaeographically be assigned to c. 10th century. This is probably assignable to Sundara Chōḷa.

It registers a gift of 25 īḻakkāśu for maintaining a perpetual lamp to the deity Tirukuḍittiṭṭai-perumāḷ of Amaninārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a dēvadāna and brahmadēya on the northern bank (of the river Kāvērī), by Vimaladēvarasa Vāsudēvabhaṭṭa-Sōmāsi of Araṇaipuram. Also records an endowment of some īḻakkāśu (figure lost) for a similar perpetual lamp by Chandraśēkaraṉ Tirumoyaḍi ••• of Paṉaiyūr.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī ko Irācakecari paṉmarkki yāṇṭu 10 5āvatu vaṭakarait tevatāṉa brahmateyam Amaninārāyaṇa caturvvetimaṅkalattu tirukkuṭuttiṭṭai perumāḷukku nontāviḷakku cantrāticcavaṟ Erikka Araṇaipurattu vimalatevarasa
vāsudevabhaṭṭa somayāciyār Oru nontāviḷakku vaiytta Īḻakkācu 2 10 5 paṉaiyūruṭaiyāṉ śantraśekaraṉ tirumoy Aṭik Oru nontā viḷakku cantrāticcavaṟ Erikka vaicca ĪThe inscription stops here abruptly.
Digital edition of SII 25.300 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
